expectations.text: Classifies the system as high-risk via the Art. 6(1) path: safety component of a product covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation (MDR) that undergoes third-party conformity assessment
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'your AI system is classified as high-risk under Article 6(1) of the EU AI Act — this is the "Annex I product-safety route"' and identifies MDR as 'Annex I, Section A' harmonisation legislation requiring notified-body assessment.

expectations.text: States the compliance deadline for Annex I embedded high-risk systems as 2 August 2028 (as amended by the Digital Omnibus; originally 2 August 2027). Any deadline stated must be Aug 2028.
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'the applicable AI Act compliance deadline is 2 August 2027 — this is the later of the Act's staggered deadlines, specifically set aside for Annex I product-safety high-risk AI systems.' This is the outdated pre-Digital-Omnibus date; the assertion requires any stated deadline to be Aug 2028. No mention of the Digital Omnibus or the deferred 2028 date anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Explains the integrated conformity route: AI Act requirements are assessed within the existing MDR notified-body procedure (Art. 43(3)) rather than a separate parallel assessment, with a single set of technical documentation permitted
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'the AI Act's high-risk requirements... must be built into the notified body's conformity assessment under the MDR, rather than duplicated as a separate AI Act certificate... intended to avoid double certification burden' and 'Single conformity assessment procedure'.

expectations.text: Identifies the dual-regulator reality: notified body under MDR plus AI Act requirements (Arts. 9-15) folded into the MDR QMS/technical documentation
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'The notified body assessing your device under MDR must also be designated/qualified... to assess conformity with the AI Act's high-risk requirements' and 'Your existing MDR QMS... needs to be extended to incorporate the AI Act's QMS elements (Article 17).'

expectations.text: Gives practical sequencing advice (align AI Act risk management with ISO 14971 processes, update technical file, involve the notified body early)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Engage your notified body early to confirm they are AI-Act-qualified... and start integrating AI Act Annex IV documentation requirements into your existing MDR technical file now, well ahead of the... deadline.' Risk management section references extending the risk file spanning both frameworks (though ISO 14971 is not named explicitly, the intent and sequencing advice is present).
